This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description: We’re looking for a hands\-on Cloud Security Analyst with deep expertise in cloud\-native and container security. This role sits at the core of securing large\-scale, multi\-cloud and Kubernetes\-driven environments. You will play a critical role in defining security standards, implementing hardened baselines, and enhancing detection and response capabilities—working closely with DevOps, Networking, and SOC teams to embed security into every layer of design and automation. 🧩 Key Responsibilities Strengthen and optimize cloud\-native security controls across IAM, network security (WAF, VPC), and data protection (KMS, Vault) Deploy and manage CSPM, CNAPP, and CWPP solutions to maintain continuous security posture across multi\-cloud and Kubernetes environments Integrate cloud telemetry (GCP Audit Logs, AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Kubernetes audit logs) into SIEM/SOAR platforms for centralized visibility Define and enforce cloud security architectures and guardrails aligned with Zero Trust and Least Privilege principles Develop and maintain Security\-as\-Code practices using Terraform, CloudFormation, or Bicep, including policy\-as\-code frameworks Own and enhance the security posture of containerized environments (GKE, AKS, EKS), including image, registry, and runtime security Harden Kubernetes clusters through RBAC, NetworkPolicies, Admission Controllers, and secure configurations Lead cloud security incident response activities including triage, containment, and forensic investigations Continuously monitor emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and attack vectors within cloud\-native ecosystems 🎯 Required Qualifications Experience 5\+ years in Information Security Minimum 3\+ years in Cloud Security At least 2\+ years working with Kubernetes / container security Cloud Expertise Hands\-on experience with at least two major cloud platforms (GCP and Azure preferred; AWS/OCI a plus) Container \& Kubernetes Security Strong understanding of Kubernetes security controls (RBAC, Secrets, Ingress, TLS) Familiarity with container runtime security and orchestration Technical Skills Proficiency in scripting (Python preferred; Bash or Go is a plus) Working knowledge of Infrastructure as Code (IaC) and automation Security Frameworks \& Compliance Knowledge of ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST, and CIS Benchmarks Certifications (Preferred) CISSP CKS (Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist) GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Microsoft Azure Security Engineer / Cybersecurity Architect AWS Certified Security – Specialty Soft Skills Strong stakeholder management and ability to influence engineering teams Experience working in global or multi\-national environments ⭐ Nice to Have Experience securing microservices, APIs, and serverless architectures Background in penetration testing or red teaming (cloud/container focus) Experience in large\-scale SaaS, networking, or cloud\-first organizations Additional certifications across GCP, Azure, AWS, or OCI Kubernetes certifications such as CKA or advanced security\-focused credentials If interested kindly share your updated cv to remya\-saras@dyneits.com

title: Digital Automation Specialist
description: **Digital Automation Specialist** We are looking for a tech savvy marketeer to join our team at Flutter UK \& Ireland. As a member of our MarTech team in Marketing Services, we want you to put automation at the heart of our workflow processes and drive forward our AI /automation capabilities across key digital marketing channels to improve efficiency, targeting, optimisation and campaign delivery. The successful candidate will work with the wider Marketing Services team to best apply operational excellence across key tooling partners in the delivery of our marketing programme across brands and verticals. **What You’ll Do** * Identify and implement AI/automation opportunities across paid media, search, social, and affiliate marketing. * Build automated workflows for campaign setup, optimisation, audience management, and reporting; in the process creating a playbook for marketing operators to iterate \& scale. * Partner with channel teams to integrate automated bidding, creative selection, and feed\-based optimisation programmes. * Work with MarTech/AdTech teams and platforms to deploy new automation features and ensure best\-practice governance in the delivery of our marketing activity. * Monitor performance and continuously refine automated processes for accuracy, scale, and efficiency. * Clearly communicate our roadmap to stakeholders, ensuring plans stay on track and successes are celebrated and results/progress shared. * Responsible for ensuring adherence to wider Flutter AI \& Automation enablement policies, ensuring safe \& privacy compliant adoption. **How You’ll Do It** * Bachelors degree in Marketing, Business Communications or Applied Computer Science (preferred) * Exposure to marketing automation platforms with familiarity of basic marketing metrics. Hands\-on experience using AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, or similar technologies in a marketing context. * Comply with responsible AI, governance frameworks, \& AI ethics. * Experience building or supporting campaign creation and customer journey * Working knowledge of HTML/CSS and operating systems * Experience working in multi\-location and cross\-functional teams in a multi brand and multi vertical business. * A good understanding of Gen AI, large language models, and how AI products work. You stay current with AI developments and can articulate how AI capabilities can solve business problems. You actively leverage Flutter’s AI tools to maximize your productivity. **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** **Extraordinary starts here.** Flutter UK \& Ireland unites a dozen brands, hundreds of teams and thousands of colleagues who create trusted entertainment for millions of customers every week. With the likes of Paddy Power, Betfair and Sky Betting \& Gaming in the fold, we’re the largest division of global entertainment giant Flutter and boast some of the most popular apps and games in UK and Ireland markets. At Flutter UK\&I, we can guarantee you that no two days will ever be the same — and you’ll have the chance to make a difference to your career, the industry, and your amazing colleagues. **What We’ll Offer You** * £1,000 annual learning fund * Unlimited holiday * Twice\-yearly bonus (with part of it guaranteed!) * Pension contribution scheme * Private healthcare * Flexible ways of working – home or office, it’s your choice! * Access to thousands of Udemy courses * Invest via the Company Sharesave Scheme * 16 hours paid volunteering time per year **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply.** **Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.**

title: E-Commerce Executive
description: About the Role We are looking for a driven and commercially minded Ecommerce Executive to join our growing apparel brands. You will take ownership of our Shopify stores, helping to drive revenue, optimise the customer journey, and support our digital growth strategy across multiple brands – Both B2B and DTC. This is a hands\-on, varied role suited to someone who thrives in a fast\-paced fashion environment and has a genuine passion for ecommerce and digital retail. **Role Overview** * **Job Title Ecommerce Executive** * **Industry Apparel / Fashion Retail** * **Location Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid considered)** * **Contract Full\-Time, Permanent** * **Experience 2–4 Years in Ecommerce** * **Reporting To Head of Digital / Ecommerce Manager** Key Responsibilities Shopify Store Management * Manage and maintain multiple Shopify stores across our apparel brands, ensuring optimal performance and user experience. * Oversee product uploads, descriptions, pricing, and imagery to keep catalogues accurate and on\-brand. * Configure and manage Shopify apps, plugins, and integrations (payments, fulfilment,reviews, etc.). * Monitor site health, resolve technical issues, and liaise with developers where needed. * Lead seasonal and promotional site updates, campaign launches, and new product drops. Trading \& Performance * Analyse and report on key ecommerce metrics including traffic, conversion rate, AOV, andrevenue. * Identify trading opportunities and recommend actions to drive sales and improve site performance. * Support the delivery of promotional calendars and ensure all commercial activity is live and accurate. * Work with the marketing team to align on\-site activity with paid, social, and email campaigns. Customer Experience \& CRO * Continuously review and optimise the customer journey across desktop and mobile. * Conduct A/B testing and make data\-driven recommendations to improve conversion. * Manage on\-site merchandising, collections, and search functionality. * Ensure the checkout experience is seamless, reducing basket abandonment. Brand \& Content * Ensure all brand stores maintain a consistent look, feel, and tone of voice. * Collaborate with creative teams on imagery, copy, and content requirements. * Support new brand or product launches end\-to\-end from a digital perspective. Social Media \& Digital Marketing * Plan and execute social media marketing activity across Instagram, TikTok, and Facebook to support brand awareness and drive traffic to our Shopify stores. * Create and manage paid digital advertising campaigns across Google Ads (Search,Shopping, Display) and Meta Ads, optimising for ROAS and conversion. * Monitor, analyse, and report on the performance of all paid and organic digital channels, making data\-led recommendations for improvement. * Work closely with the content and creative teams to develop social\-first assets that align with campaign objectives and brand guidelines. * Stay up to date with social commerce trends, platform updates, and emerging digital marketing opportunities relevant to the apparel industry. Requirements Essential * 2–4 years of hands\-on ecommerce experience, ideally within apparel or fashion retail. * Demonstrable experience managing Shopify stores at a brand or agency level. * Strong commercial acumen with the ability to translate data into actions. * Proficiency in Google Analytics (GA4\), Shopify Analytics, or similar tools. * Highly organised with excellent attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities. * Strong communication skills and ability to work cross\-functionally. * Strong eye for design, branding, and digital visual merchandising with an understanding of fashion retail aesthetics. * Experience creating and managing website layouts, promotional banners, and customer\-facing content within Shopify or similar ecommerce platforms. Desirable * Experience working across multiple brands or Shopify stores simultaneously. * Familiarity with email platforms such as Klaviyo and how they integrate with ecommerce. * Understanding of SEO best practices and on\-site optimisation. * Experience with Shopify * Knowledge of paid media channels and how they interact with onsite performance. * Proven track record running live selling events on TikTok, Instagram, or Facebook. * Experience with Google Ads (Search, Shopping) and Meta Ads Manager. * Experience using AI image generation tools (e.g. Merchd, Gemmini, ChatGPT, etc.) for commercial content creation. * Strong visual and creative judgement with exceptional attention to detail and product accuracy. * Understanding of prompt engineering and AI\-assisted content workflows within a retail or ecommerce environment. What We Offer * Generous staff discount across all our apparel brands. * A collaborative, creative team environment in a growing business. * Be at the forefront of using in\-house AI tools, working with the latest technology to enhance how we trade, create, and grow. title: Early Years Childcare Practitioner
description: **We have job openings for Full Time work Monday \- Friday 40 hours per week. Please be available for these hours before applying for the position.** We have an amazing opportunity to hire a Childcare Practitioner and add to our excellent team at Parkside Childcare! We are one of south Dublin's most established childcare facilities, operating nearly 30 years! If you haven't worked in a creche before, we will provide excellent training. **We are hiring Childcare Practitioners with QQI Level 5,6, 7 and 8 in Early Year's/ Childcare Education. All salaries are based on qualification and years of experience in a early years childcare setting.** You **must** have an Early Years Education qualification recognised by DCYA in Ireland to work in an Irish Childcare setting. Check if your qualification is recognised here : https://www.gov.ie/en/publication/22405\-early\-years\-recognised\-qualifications/ **What we offer to all employees :** * Competitive salary * 21 days annual leave, 2 company days, 1/2 day on your Birthday * Anniversary Bonus * Referral bonus for introducing new colleagues * Bike to work scheme * Sick Pay * Overtime @1\.5 * Continuing Professional Development (CPD) * Training provided \- manual handling , fire safety, allergens * Support in Education progression * Progression opportunities within the company **Duties \& Responsibilities of a Childcare Practitioner :** * Delivering high\-quality care and learning opportunities for the children in our care. * Implementation of the Aistear Curriculum while upholding the Siolta Standards. * Observe, monitor and record the development of the children in compliance with the Childcare (Pre\-School Services) Regulations 2016 and be familiar with the Regulations and the Quality Regulatory Framework. * Ensure the health and safety of the children. * Support the children’s development, learning \& play. * Communicate with the parents on the development of their child in a professional, effective and friendly capacity. **Requirements from Candidates :** * ***Minimum*** Fetac/QQI Level 5 in **Early Years Education.** Additional experience and qualifications are highly desirable * Clear Garda Vetting (and International police clearance if having lived outside of Ireland for more than **6 months)** is required. * Two written references, one from your most recent employer, that will be verified. Candidates should take into consideration travel and commute to **South County Dublin** If you are interested in learning from the best and joining a super team, we would be delighted to hear from you! Salary will be competitive and commensurate with experience. Please submit a full CV with **no time gaps.** Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Graduate Pay: From €15\.00 per hour Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee discount * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you currently based in Dublin? Experience: * Childcare: 3 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Level 5\+ Early Year Education or a letter from DCEDIY? (required) Work Location: In person

title: Executive Assistance
description: **Summary** We are seeking a highly proactive, strategic, and seasoned Executive Assistant to partner with our senior leaders and drive operational excellence across our global teams. Based in our Dublin office, you will serve as the central anchor for leaders and teams spanning Ireland, Poland, and North America (US/Toronto). **Job Description** An Executive Assistant at our company acts as a trusted business partner who anticipates needs, manages complex cross\-border logistics, and supports business objectives for a highly distributed organization. If you are a skilled prioritizer who thrives in a fast\-paced, global environment, we want to hear from you. Location: **In\-Office\- Dublin, Ireland** (Hybrid policy applies: minimum 3 days per week in our Dublin office, with flexibility for occasional remote work). Employment Type: Full\-time What you’ll do * Strategic Calendar \& Time Management: Manage complex, high\-volume calendars across multiple conflicting time zones (GMT, CET, EST). Prioritize critical meetings, resolve conflicts independently, and safeguard leaders' time for strategic priorities. * Operational Support \& Coordination – Act as the primary coordination hub for executives, managing scheduling requests, meeting preparation, shared documentation, and cross\-regional alignment with other assistants. * Global Travel \& Expense Management: End\-to\-end planning of complex international multi\-leg travel itineraries (particularly between Ireland, Poland, and North America), ensuring alignment with local compliance, tax, and visa requirements. Manage and reconcile expenses efficiently. * Onsite Meetings, Events, and Team Offsites – Plan and run onsite meetings, workshops, and offsites end\-to\-end, including logistics and visitor management, to support PDO priorities and create seamless in\-person experiences. * Confidentiality \& Discretion: Act as a gatekeeper for highly sensitive, confidential business information, exercising impeccable judgment at all times. * Admin Community Leadership: Partner with and mentor junior administrative staff, contributing to the global EA community within the company to share best practices and ensure cross\-coverage. What you’ll bring * Experience: 5\+ years of administrative support or executive assistant experience, with a proven track record of supporting senior management (VP/Director level) within a fast\-growing global tech or multinational environment. * Global Matrix Competency: Strong experience supporting distributed or remote teams across European and North American time zones. You understand how to bridge cultural and logistical gaps. * Project Management Skills: Ability to manage multiple activities simultaneously, handle ambiguity with grace, and pivot seamlessly under pressure. * Communication Mastery: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English; ability to interface confidently with executives and cross\-functional partners worldwide. * Tech\-Savviness: Advanced proficiency with Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Docs/Sheets/Slides), Slack, Zoom, and modern project management tools (e.g., Asana, Jira, Mondaydotcom). You leverage technology to automate and streamline work. * Problem\-Solving Mindset: A highly autonomous ownership mindset. You don’t just flag issues; you come to the table with potential solutions. Nice to have * Experience supporting Engineering, Product, or highly technical global operations teams. * Familiarity with Polish business culture or basic language skills (though English is our primary business language). * Prior experience navigating complex corporate travel and expense policies across EMEA and AMER. Work hours and travel * Hours: Core hours are aligned with the Dublin business day, but this role requires deliberate flexibility to accommodate early/late touchpoints with Poland (CET) and the US/Toronto (EST). * Travel: Occasional international travel (approximately 10\-15%) to Poland or North America for key leadership summits or team events. Success looks like * Operational Synergy: Leaders' priorities are seamlessly executed with zero friction, and global team alignment feels effortless despite time zone barriers. * Flawless Execution: High\-stakes meetings and multi\-regional events are executed on budget, on time, and yield clear, actionable follow\-ups. * Proactive Influence: You are recognized as an indispensable business partner who optimizes operations, structures chaotic environments, and builds a cohesive culture across Dublin, Poland, and the US. **About Guidewire** Guidewire is the platform P\&C insurers trust to engage, innovate, and grow efficiently. We combine digital, core, analytics, and AI to deliver our platform as a cloud service. More than 540\+ insurers in 40 countries, from new ventures to the largest and most complex in the world, run on Guidewire. As a partner to our customers, we continually evolve to enable their success. We are proud of our unparalleled implementation track record with 1600\+ successful projects, supported by the largest R\&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. Our Marketplace provides hundreds of applications that accelerate integration, localization, and innovation. For more information, please visit www.guidewire.com and follow us on Twitter: @Guidewire\_PandC. Guidewire Software, Inc. is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to an inclusive workplace, and believe that a diversity of perspectives, abilities, and cultures is a key to our success. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, marital status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability. All offers are contingent upon passing a criminal history and other background checks where it's applicable to the position.

title: Expansion Event Coordinator
description: ***Join Tether and Shape the Future of Digital Finance*** At Tether, we’re not just building products, we’re pioneering a global financial revolution. Our cutting\-edge solutions empower businesses—from exchanges and wallets to payment processors and ATMs—to seamlessly integrate reserve\-backed tokens across blockchains. By harnessing the power of blockchain technology, Tether enables you to store, send, and receive digital tokens instantly, securely, and globally, all at a fraction of the cost. Transparency is the bedrock of everything we do, ensuring trust in every transaction. ***Innovate with Tether*** **Tether Finance:** Our innovative product suite features the world’s most trusted stablecoin, **USDT**, relied upon by hundreds of millions worldwide, alongside pioneering digital asset tokenization services. But that’s just the beginning: **Tether Power:** Driving sustainable growth, our energy solutions optimize excess power for Bitcoin mining using eco\-friendly practices in state\-of\-the\-art, geo\-diverse facilities. **Tether Data:** Fueling breakthroughs in AI and peer\-to\-peer technology, we reduce infrastructure costs and enhance global communications with cutting\-edge solutions like **KEET**, our flagship app that redefines secure and private data sharing. **Tether Education**: Democratizing access to top\-tier digital learning, we empower individuals to thrive in the digital and gig economies, driving global growth and opportunity. **Tether Evolution**: At the intersection of technology and human potential, we are pushing the boundaries of what is possible, crafting a future where innovation and human capabilities merge in powerful, unprecedented ways. ***Why Join Us?*** Our team is a global talent powerhouse, working remotely from every corner of the world. If you’re passionate about making a mark in the fintech space, this is your opportunity to collaborate with some of the brightest minds, pushing boundaries and setting new standards. We’ve grown fast, stayed lean, and secured our place as a leader in the industry. If you have excellent English communication skills and are ready to contribute to the most innovative platform on the planet, Tether is the place for you. **Are you ready to be part of the future?** **About the job** We are looking for a highly organized and culturally agile Expansion Events Coordinator to drive the physical presence of our Expansion Team. In this role you will be the operational backbone for our international growth, serving as a critical partner to our Expansion Managers in each country. You will play a pivotal role in the end\-to\-end execution of a diverse event portfolio, ranging from conferences, workshops, summits and brand sponsorships to large\-scale international conferences. Notably, you will provide direct support for the department, ensuring these world\-class gatherings are executed with precision. **Responsibilities** * **End\-to\-End Execution:** Lead the coordination of international events from initial concept and booth design through to on\-site execution and post\-event reporting. * **Stakeholder Management:** Act as the primary liaison between global venues, vendors, and internal stakeholders to ensure seamless service delivery. * **Logistics \& Operations**: Master the "fine print" by managing complex calendars, scheduling cross\-functional meetings, overseeing intricate travel, event logistics, Finance \& Budget coordination. * **Financial Oversight**: Support the Expansion Team with budget reconciliation, expense tracking, and detailed performance reporting. * **Internal Logistics**: Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate travel logistics. * **Global Communication**: Manage both internal and external communications, ensuring the brand voice is consistent across English and Spanish\-speaking markets. * Bachelor’s Degree in Event Management or Bachelor’s Degree in Hospitality \& Tourism Management * A willingness and availability to travel when required. * Experience: 5\+ years of proven success in global corporate events or the professional event industry. * Linguistic Versatility: Native or full professional proficiency in English and Spanish is required. (Bonus points if you speak Italian). * Tech\-Savviness: Advanced proficiency in Google Suite and Microsoft Office, with the ability to quickly adopt new project management tools. * A solid grasp of budgeting, invoicing, and expense management. * Adaptability: A "no task too big or small" mindset, comfortable moving from high\-level sponsorship strategy to the ground\-level execution. **Important information for candidates** Recruitment scams have become increasingly common. To protect yourself, please keep the following in mind when applying for roles: * **Apply only through our official channels.** We do not use third\-party platforms or agencies for recruitment unless clearly stated. All open roles are listed on our official careers page: https://tether.recruitee.com/ * **Verify the recruiter’s identity.** All our recruiters have verified LinkedIn profiles. If you’re unsure, you can confirm their identity by checking their profile or contacting us through our website. * **Be cautious of unusual communication methods.** We do not conduct interviews over WhatsApp, Telegram, or SMS. All communication is done through official company emails and platforms. * **Double\-check email addresses.** All communication from us will come from emails ending in **@**tether.to **or @**tether.io * **We will never request payment or financial details.** If someone asks for personal financial information or payment at any point during the hiring process, it is a scam. Please report it immediately. **When in doubt, feel free to reach out through our official website.**

title: FaST Product Install Engineer (f/m/d)
description: **Company Overview** KLA is a global leader in diversified electronics for the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. Virtually every electronic device in the world is produced using our technologies. No laptop, smartphone, wearable device, voice\-controlled gadget, flexible screen, VR device or smart car would have made it into your hands without us. KLA invents systems and solutions for the manufacturing of wafers and reticles, integrated circuits, packaging, printed circuit boards and flat panel displays. The innovative ideas and devices that are advancing humanity all begin with inspiration, research and development. KLA focuses more than average on innovation and we invest 15% of sales back into R\&D. Our expert teams of physicists, engineers, data scientists and problem\-solvers work together with the world’s leading technology providers to accelerate the delivery of tomorrow’s electronic devices. Life here is exciting and our teams thrive on tackling really hard problems. There is never a dull moment with us. **Group/Division** The KLA Services team headquartered in Milpitas, CA is our service organization that consists of Service Sales and Marketing, Spares Supply Chain management, Field Operations, Engineering, Product Training, and Technical Support. The KLA Services organization partners with our field teams and customers in all business sectors to maintain the high performance and productivity of our products through a flexible portfolio of services. Our comprehensive services include: proactive management of tools to identify and improve performance; expertise in optics, image processing and motion control with worldwide service engineers, 24/7 technical support teams and knowledge management systems; and an extensive parts network to ensure worldwide availability of parts. **Job Description/Preferred Qualifications** Do you want to work on some of the most complex and cutting edge technologies in a dynamic environment with good career opportunities, if so, KLA is searching for Engineers to join our installation team in Europe securing proper tool installation at KLA customer sites \& completing all activities required to ensure a KLA tool functions accurately! Our Installation engineers are a critical part of our new product deployment projects while traveling to some of the most advanced factories in the world they work in multicultural teams to meet our project commitments. **Key** **responsibilities include:** * Hold pre\-installation meetings with the customer to scope customer unique requirements. Create a plan of action to measure/confirm/satisfy unique customer needs. Validate deliverables with customers. * Install tool at customer site to meet KLA specifications, through system testing according to procedures. This includes but is not limited to: light source and optical integration and precision alignment; wafer handler robot integration and teaching; camera and collection optics alignment and calibration; system\-level calibrations and verification; performance testing for sensitivity and repeatability; stability and cleanliness testing; applications verification and product wafer validation. * Solving complex system issues in a timely manner, using abstract thought, data analysis, seven\-step problem\-solving methodology, and other available tools. * Management of critical issues across customers and regions. * After system testing, meet with the customer to facilitate verification of the tool performance, during which the validation of the tool is performed, to ensure it is properly functioning. * Management of customer satisfaction through pre\-activity meetings, routine update meetings, escalation meetings, post\-activity meetings, and warranty meetings. * Perform install/customer\-focused application testing to approve the operational quality of the tool. * Provide new product installation training to worldwide customer support engineering. KLA offers a challenging and multinational environment at the forefront of high technology and a highly competitive package. As a global organization, the KLA family allows you to build a career within and avail of a variety of opportunities across our technologies and factories around the globe. Joining KLA could be one of the best decisions you will ever make! As an Equal Opportunity Employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability, or any other characteristics protected by applicable law. **Minimum Qualifications** * Technical degree/diploma in engineering, mechanical, electrical, electronic engineering or equivalent work experience. * 2\-3 years semiconductor industry work experience is a bonus. * Panel of competencies in optical, mechanic, electronic, lithography, metrology, and laser technology – is desirable. * Good level technical \& analytical skills, ability to understand and solve problems in software and electronic, optic, mechanical, electro\-mechanical or electro\-optical systems. * Knowledge of computer theory, operating systems i.e. MS Windows. * Good interpersonal/communication skills. * Ability to work under pressure and work to project deadlines. * Available to travel internationally. *We offer a competitive, family friendly total rewards package. We design our programs to reflect our commitment to an inclusive environment, while ensuring we provide benefits that meet the diverse needs of our employees.* *KLA is proud to be an equal opportunity employer* Be aware of potentially fraudulent job postings or suspicious recruiting activity by persons that are currently posing as KLA employees. KLA never asks for any financial compensation to be considered for an interview, to become an employee, or for equipment. Further, KLA does not work with any recruiters or third parties who charge such fees either directly or on behalf of KLA. Please ensure that you have searched KLA’s Careers website for legitimate job postings. KLA follows a recruiting process that involves multiple interviews in person or on video conferencing with our hiring managers. If you are concerned that a communication, an interview, an offer of employment, or that an employee is not legitimate, please send an email to talent.acquisition@kla.com to confirm the person you are communicating with is an employee. We take your privacy very seriously and confidentially handle your information.

title: Field Sales Representatives - Meath/Louth
description: #### **Your mission** **Join the Electric Ireland Sales Team – Earn Big, Grow Fast, Be Valued** ------------------------------------------------------------------------- Are you ambitious, outgoing, and ready to build a career? Join the Electric Ireland Sales Team, representing Ireland’s leading electricity supplier. Whether you’re just starting out or looking for your next big move, we’ll set you up to succeed — with great pay, full training, and career growth from day one. ### **What You’ll Earn** * €14\.15 Per hour * Annual : €29,400 * €8,000\+ commission potential (realistic OTE €37,000\+) * Commission from Day One – no thresholds to hit before you start earning * Weekly pay direct to your account ### **What You Get** * Working hours: Monday to Friday, 11 AM–8 PM (no weekends) * 30 paid days off every year (20 days annual leave \+ 10 paid bank holidays) * Career progression: full training, regular coaching, and clear paths for promotion * Supportive team culture that helps you grow and succeed ### **What You’ll Do** * Visit residential customers face\-to\-face * Promote Electric Ireland’s energy plans in a clear and honest way * Guide customers through a simple switch and earn commission on every sale * Represent a trusted, household name with professionalism and pride #### **Your profile** ### **What We’re Looking For** * A positive, can\-do attitude and willingness to learn * Strong people skills — you enjoy talking to others * Ambition to succeed and meet personal and team goals * No experience needed — we hire for attitude * **A car and driving licence is required** ### **Ready to Apply?** If you're ready to earn well, build a meaningful career, and have fun doing it, we want to hear from you. We’re known for our positive culture, strong leadership, and commitment to training and development. Our teams have been representing leading brands in Ireland for over 30 years.* *We value our employees and help them grow through training and development opportunities. Our onboarding process sets you up for success, whether you're based in Dublin, Belfast, or out in the field. If you're passionate and enthusiastic about your career, FMI is the place for you.*

title: Field Service Technician - Talent Pool (Dublin)
description: **About Miele** How does a family\-owned company become the global market leader in the premium segment for domestic appliances and a trusted partner for solutions in commercial and medical technology? By striving to always do better and to keep improving. This mindset defines what it means to work at Miele and drives the success of our products. Our approximately 23,000 employees can rely on one thing: those who embrace our commitment to quality will grow – both personally and professionally. **​** **Talent Pool: Domestic Premium Appliance Field Engineer (Dublin)** **Please note, this is a Talent Pool and not a live vacancy. You are registering your interest in Field Service Technician opportunities in the Dublin area.** *Opportunities will be full\-time (40 hours per week) with the option to work this across 4 or 5 days a week.* The Miele Service team is an award\-winning service delivery function, supporting the needs of our domestic customers directly in their homes. We hire the best technicians from the white goods sector to join our field service community. Our team is built on a legacy of excellence and innovation that dates back to 1899\. We are proud to offer a workplace and culture that is as future proof as our world\-renowned products. At Miele, we don’t just build appliances—we build careers. Join us in living our philosophy of being ‘Immer Besser’—Forever Better. **About the role** Working independently, we give you the tools to succeed. We build a community around you to share your practices and feel part of something special, inspiring results as a team with a family feel, and redefining excellence every day. Our Domestic Field Service teams work creatively to service our customers in their homes, bringing a new challenge every day as you diagnose and solve the issues that arise. You could be servicing any of our world class products to sustain their life and support our sustainability promise. As one of our brand ambassadors, you will be encouraged and rewarded to promote our care product and appliances. we have a reward platform where you can earn points to exchange for personal gift cards or products. **About you** Ideally you will have worked in an Appliance engineer or Appliance technician role however, if you bring technical experience from any industry, what would be advantageous is if it was gained in the electronics, white goods, electrical or gas industry. We are flexible on you background and can offer full training on our appliances. In turn we will support your training to achieve great results in line with our “Immer Besser” philosophy. Alongside your existing skills and experience, we’re looking for engaged team players who will collaborate, innovate and use their passion to drive an exceptional customer experience at every touchpoint. A gas qualification and experience would be highly desirable but not essential . **What to expect from us:** In addition to a strong commitment to your personal development and growth, we can offer you on target earnings of up to €51,396\.25, along with a comprehensive benefits package: • Starting salary of €42,128\.33 which increases incrementally as you gain skills. • Potential to rise within the first 8 months. • A daily lunch allowance included in salary. • Performance related bonus scheme. • Commission for introducing our customers to products, accessories and care collection. • Optional private use of Company Vehicle subject to relevant HMRC rates. • Contributory pension scheme. • Excellent training and support network. • Active personal development plan and opportunities to further your career. • Substantial discount on all company products. title: Graduate Architect / Architect
description: A growing architectural design studio in South Dublin is seeking an **Architect / Graduate Architect** \- working towards registration with local experience, to join their expanding team. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Architect / Graduate Architect with 2 years\+ experience to join a collaborative team to work across a diverse project portfolio including commercial, retail, mixed\-use, and residential developments throughout Ireland. The ideal Architect / Graduate Architect will have strong technical and design skills, proficiency in BIM (ArchiCAD OR Revit) previous experience taking projects from planning through construction, and a solid understanding of current Irish building regulations. **\*Excellent benefits package and hybrid working options on offer\*** **Desired Skills and Experience:** * RIAI or RIBA membership (or actively working towards registration) * 2\+ years’ post‑qualification experience * Strong knowledge of Building Regulations and BCAR * Experience across residential, commercial, retail, or mixed‑use projects * Proficiency in BIM \- ArchiCAD / Revit * Planning‑to‑construction project delivery experience * Strong communication, coordination, and organisational skills * On‑site experience is highly desired * **Eligibility to work in Ireland (essential)** **Salary \& Benefits** * €40k–€55k DOE * Hybrid working – excellent work / life balance * Generous holiday allowance and company days * Pension, Bonus, Professional subscriptions If you are a **Registered Architect / Graduate Architect** nearing registration, and would like to learn more about this role or similar opportunities across Ireland, please send your CV to Deborah Byrne.

title: Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia)
description: We are seeking to recruit a Graduate Business Solutions Developer (Multimedia) to join our creative and technical team in producing engaging, interactive digital learning content for our extensive global client base. This role offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the design and development of high\-quality front\-end multimedia solutions that support over a million active corporate learners each year. The position is based in our Dublin office and provides an excellent foundation for a graduate looking to begin their career within a leading technology\-driven learning organisation. The ideal candidate will be a creative, curious, and motivated graduate with an understanding of front\-end and multimedia design principles and a passion for crafting visually compelling digital experiences. They should bring problem solving abilities, an eagerness to learn new tools and techniques, and a collaborative mindset. Attention to detail, a proactive approach, and effective communication skills will be essential in delivering polished, user\-centred solutions while continuously expanding their technical and creative capabilities. The successful candidate will work closely with an international team of instructional designers, software engineers, project managers, and client\-facing teams to deliver innovative multimedia content and interactive learning experiences. They will play a key role in transforming complex ideas into intuitive, engaging digital assets that support our clients’ goals in learning, performance improvement, and digital transformation worldwide. **This role involves:** •Transforming learning designs into interactive digital media optimised for phones, tablets, web browsers, and emerging AI\-enabled interfaces. •Using technologies such as Angular, Node.js, and Python as part this work to output intuitive user interfaces and user experiences in HTML, JavaScript, and CSS. •Influencing and advising cross\-functional teams, sharing ideas and contributing to collaborative project outcomes. •Creating high\-quality digital learning content using industry\-standard tools like Articulate Rise and Articulate Storyline. •Diagnosing, fixing, and troubleshooting issues across both traditional and AI\-enabled experiences to ensure smooth, reliable learner journeys. •Preparing and working with content and structured data for use with automation and AI systems such as Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. •Ensuring the accurate and effective implementation of digital media, maintaining consistently high standards of quality. •Testing and validating digital content across multiple platforms and Learning Management Systems, ensuring flawless performance. •Keeping up to date with fast\-evolving eLearning, multimedia, and online technology trends, bringing fresh ideas to the team. **Additional Skills \& Attributes:** •Curiosity and enthusiasm for new tools, technologies, modern development practices, conversational interfaces, AI\-assisted user experiences, and how design choices influence AI behaviour. •Willingness to continually learn and improve. •Strong attention to detail supported by an analytical mindset. •Effective communication skills and a collaborative working style. •Strong logical thinking and problem\-solving ability. •Appreciation of and interest in good design, both technically and visually. •Ability to think clearly about structure, consistency, and constraints when working with digital content. **Minimum requirements:** •Bachelor’s or master’s degree in a field related to multimedia, computer science, web development, or programming. •Exposure through academic or personal projects to data\-driven applications, conversational interfaces, or AI\-assisted tools is an advantage, but not required. •A good understanding of interface design, digital graphics or digital media, video, and audio. Intuition is an Equal Opportunities Employer

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: **Job Description** Are you an enthusiastic **Graduate Electrical Engineer** ? IPS is hiring! Join our industry\-leading team at our European office based in **Dublin, Ireland** and become part of our enriching Graduate Programme. The Graduate Electrical Engineer will undertake specific tasks on project teams using their education, creativity and expertise to resolve engineering problems and tasks as they are assigned. A pool of knowledge and experience from all of the IPS teams across the world will be available to our graduates and, consequently, more experienced staff will always be available when a graduate engineer needs support. **Job Duties And Responsibilities** * As a graduate Electrical Engineer, you will get the opportunity to work on world class projects for global clients within the Life Sciences sector. * Work with a professional team of top industry experts in the delivery of complex facilities * Participate in engineering design reviews (GMP, safety, operational, maintenance) * Use computer assisted engineering and design software and equipment to prepare engineering and design documents * Research design options and documents findings for Senior Engineer and Line Manager * Research code issues and ask general questions of code officials * Ensure that drawings and designs are in accordance with IPS engineering and design standards * Promote and apply Health and Safety in all aspects of Electrical Design and Construction * Support design computations and quantity estimates for the smaller, less complex projects within the business unit (low voltage systems, fire alarm systems, backup power generation, electrical power distribution, life safety systems, etc.) * Support with calculations using engineering formulas and skills for design such as lighting calculations, power distribution calculations, equipment sizing, etc. * Keep Project Manager informed of workload and any changes in project scope or additional services * Interface with engineers in our global network of offices * Support selecting equipment based on recommendations of Project Designer/Engineer and fills out equipment schedules. * Organise and maintain project files. Maintains a neat and organised work area which allows efficient access to information required by other team members. * Have a can\-do attitude and takes ownership of projects under the supervision of a Senior Electrical Engineer. **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Level 8 Hons Degree in Electrical Services Engineering or Electrical Engineering (preferably a Masters degree). * Proven skills in computer operation for engineering design programs, Revit and computer aided drafting. * Some previous engineering/design experience would be desirable. * Desire to work towards achieving CEng with Engineers Ireland/CIBSE/I Mech E. * Desire to contribute to the development of Electrical Engineering Team within IPS * Good interpersonal skills and demonstrates the ability to work within multi discipline team All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually, with video required. **About Us** IPS, a Berkshire Hathaway Company, is a global leader in developing innovative business solutions for the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industries. Through operational expertise and industry\-leading knowledge, skill, and passion, IPS provides consultancy services, architecture, engineering, project controls, construction management, and compliance services that allow clients to develop and manufacture life\-impacting products. Its newest acquisition, Linesight, specializes in cost, schedule, risk, program, and project management services in various market sectors, including data centers, life sciences, and high\-tech industries. With the addition of Linesight, IPS has over 3,000 professionals in over 45 offices across 17 countries in the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, Southeast Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. Specialties Process Architecture, Process Design, Engineering, Construction, Commissioning, Qualification, LEAN Project Delivery, Strategic Master Planning, Energy Conservation Management, Project Controls, Program/Project Management, Operations/Logistics Strategy and Planning, EPCMV and Staffing Services. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment at IPS without regard to race; creed; color; religion; national origin; sex; age; disability; sexual orientation; gender identity or expression; genetic predisposition or carrier status; veteran, marital, or citizenship status; or any other status protected by law. IPS is proud to be an affirmative action and equal opportunity employer. *Attention Search Firms / Third\-Party Recruiters: IPS is not seeking assistance or accepting unsolicited resumes from search firms for employment opportunities with IPS. Regardless of past practice, all resumes submitted by search firms to any employee at IPS via email, the Internet, or directly to hiring managers at IPS in any form without a valid written search agreement in place for a specific position will be deemed the sole property of IPS, and no fee will be paid in the event a candidate is hired by IPS as a result of the referral or through other means.* Connect With Us! If you do not meet the qualifications for this specific role, but would like to submit your resume for general consideration, please click here!

title: Graduate Field Service Engineer -Biomedical / GMP Focus
description: **Role Overview** This is an excellent opportunity for a Biomedical Science (or related life sciences) graduate to begin a career as a Field Service Engineer, supporting a range of high\-end laboratory instrumentation used in regulated environments. The role combines hands\-on technical work with customer interaction and ongoing specialist training, making it ideal for candidates with a strong laboratory background and exposure to Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP). You will be responsible for delivering service activities including installation, maintenance, repair, and technical support to customers across the pharmaceutical, biotechnology, academic, and government sectors. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform installation, routine maintenance, and repair of laboratory instrumentation * Support qualification activities (IQ/OQ) in line with GMP requirements and ensure accurate documentation * Provide on\-site and remote technical support (phone/email) to customers * Troubleshoot instrument and application issues in regulated laboratory environments * Work independently in the field while collaborating with the wider Service Team * Manage and coordinate your own schedule in line with customer and team requirements * Maintain accurate service and compliance records using internal systems * Ensure all work is carried out in accordance with GMP and relevant regulatory standards * Complete monthly expense reports and service documentation * Participate in product and compliance training (in\-house and manufacturer\-led, including potential international travel) **Equipment You’ll Work With** * Capillary electrophoresis systems * Robotic liquid handling systems * Flow cytometers * Centrifugation equipment * Particle and cell characterisation systems **Candidate Profile** **Essential:** * Degree in Biomedical Science, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, or related life sciences discipline * Understanding of Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) and working in regulated environments * Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities * Good interpersonal and communication skills * Self\-motivated with the ability to work independently * Strong IT/PC skills * Full driving licence and access to own car **Desirable:** * Hands\-on experience in a GMP or regulated laboratory (e.g., internships, placements, or industry experience) * Familiarity with analytical or laboratory instrumentation * Experience with qualification/validation documentation (IQ/OQ/PQ) **Training \& Development** * Structured onboarding and technical training programme * Ongoing mentoring from experienced engineers and application specialists * Manufacturer\-led training (including opportunities to travel internationally) * Development of both technical engineering skills and regulatory/GMP expertise **Package \& Benefits** * Competitive salary with bonus scheme * Private healthcare * Contributory pension scheme * 25 days annual leave * Company laptop, mobile phone, tools, and test equipment provided * Monthly car allowance and expenses **Why This Role?** This position offers a strong foundation for a career in field service within the life sciences sector, combining your laboratory and GMP knowledge with technical engineering skills. You’ll gain exposure to cutting\-edge instrumentation while working closely with leading pharmaceutical and research organisations.

title: Graduate Pest Control Technician
description: Job Title: Graduate Pest Control Technician Graduate Technician reports to: The graduate technician’s immediate superior is our Service Manager Martin Kelly. Martin will be the main point of contact, day in and day out. Graduate Technician Job Overview Ultimate Pest Control is currently seeking a motivated and enthusiastic graduate technician to join our team. This is a trainee position. Therefore, full pest control training will be provided. This position carries a high degree of responsibility. Therefore, the ideal candidate must be a self\-starter capable of working in an autonomous role. Consequently being wholly responsible for planning your own workday and servicing your customer list. There is no such thing as a typical day for a pest control technician. Therefore, it is a very interesting career. That will certainly test you. But also gives a sense of satisfaction. However, it involves dealing with a multitude of pests. So, it is not a role for the faint\-hearted. Although it is very rewarding. As you will be truly helping every customer. By resolving or preventing pest issues. Responsibilities * Carry out routine domestic and commercial pest inspections * Service all customers as per the agreed schedule * Identify any and all causes for potential pest infestations within all premises * Effectively communicate with customers and be able to recommend solutions for potential issues * Complete any minor proofing works * Complete detailed service reports following every inspection * Respond to all phone calls immediately * Carry out all emergency calls within 24\-hours * Maintain a clean van at all times * Ensure professionalism at all times * Maintain the high level of customer satisfaction that UPC has cultivated over the past few years * QualificationsThe ideal candidate must posses the following: * A full, clean driving license * Fluent English * A strong work ethic * Be a self\-starter with the ability to manage a daily schedule * Excellent time management * Stong communication skills * Excellent interpersonal skills Benefits * Starting salary of €28,5000\. * Following successful completion of exams, wages will increase * Become a qualified Pest Control Technician * Full training provided. Additionally, UPC covers the training and exams costs * Company van provided * Fuel card provided * Company phone provided * Commission and overtime available Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Graduate Pay: From €28,500\.00 per year Benefits: * Company car Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you willing to undergo a background check? * This role is only open to applicants 25 years and older due to Motor Insurance Requirements Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * A Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Physiotherapist (FIREFIT Physio & Performance)
description: FIREFIT is one of South Dublin's strongest training communities, with 500\+ active members and a multidisciplinary team of 20\+ professionals across full\-time and part\-time roles \- including Physios, Athletic Therapists, Nutritionists, and Strength \& Conditioning coaches. We're built around coaching standards, community, and long\-term progress. FIREFIT Physio \& Performance is our specialised arm, delivering a tailored, personal approach to rehab and performance. We provide 1:1 Physiotherapy, structured rehab plans that integrate with real training, and specialised services including our Masters (Over\-55s), Youth Development pathway, Group Programmes and Runners Strength \& Conditioning / Rehab. This side of the business is scaling quickly \- and being elevated at the same time. We're now hiring a Graduate Physiotherapist \- on either a full\-time or part\-time basis \- who values evidence\-based care, clear communication, and helping people return to training with confidence. **About this role** This is a blended role across coaching \+ clinic, with a clear pathway to build a strong caseload. We're hiring on both a full\-time and part\-time basis \- we'll shape the days and hours around what suits you, and the structure below scales to either. * **General class coaching \+ 1:1 appointments:** You'll coach within our general classes while also delivering 1:1 appointments. Initially coaching on the floor allows you to build trust quickly, meet members consistently, and typically leads to a high number of initial appointments booked. Over time, as your caseload grows, the role progresses into more dedicated clinic hours. * **Specialist group coaching (Physio \& Performance team only):** You'll coach FIREFIT Physio \& Performance sessions such as our Masters Programme, Youth Development Sessions and Runners Strength \& Conditioning / Rehab programmes. These specialised classes are delivered exclusively by the Physio \& Performance team to ensure clinical\-level coaching standards and continuity for clients. * **Own a niche area:** We're keen to support you in developing a specific niche and becoming the go\-to person for it \- for example women's health, golf\-specific rehab/S\&C, youth athletic development, runners strength \& conditioning, or another performance niche aligned with your strengths and passion. * **Weekly CPD \+ mentorship:** Structured weekly CPD with James Greene (Clinic Director), including case discussions, clinical reasoning and clinic/gym based application. **What we're looking for** * Qualified Physiotherapist (CORU registered/eligible) * Strong interest (or proven experience) in MSK assessment, rehabilitation, and strength \& conditioning principles * Excellent communication skills and a high standard of client care * A collaborative, proactive mindset \- comfortable working alongside coaches, clinicians, and support staff **What success looks like at FIREFIT** FIREFIT Physio \& Performance is rapidly growing \- and we're looking for key players. This role will suit someone who wants to push the business forward, contribute to raising standards, and take ownership of specific areas within the clinic. With prime locations and an already brilliant team, there's a genuine opportunity to help grow FIREFIT Physio \& Performance into one of Dublin's leading options for training\-led rehab and performance care. **How to apply** Send your CV and a short note outlining: * Whether you're interested in a full\-time or part\-time role (and, if part\-time, your general availability) * Your qualifications/registration and work experience * The clients you most enjoy working with * Any niche interests you would be interested in developing (such as women's health, running, golf S\&C, youth, performance rehab, etc.) Job Type: Full\-time Pay: Up to €50,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Gym membership * On\-site gym Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Power Apps Specialist- Dublin/ Kildare
description: **Position: Graduate** Power Applications Specialist\- Dublin/ Kildare **Location:** Dublin/Kildare **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Overview of the Post** We are seeking a knowledgeable and versatile specialist to enhance our digital solutions using MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint, and Power Apps including Model driven Applications. **Key Responsibilities:** MS Dynamics 365 Management: * Implement and configure MS Dynamics 365 and Power Applications tailored solutions to meet business requirements. * Collaborate with stakeholders to understand needs and translate them into functional specifications. * Provide ongoing support, maintenance, and enhancements to existing Dynamics 365 applications. Power Apps and Model\-Driven Apps: * Develop and customize Power Apps and Model\-Driven Apps to enhance business processes. * Integrate Power Applications with MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint, and other data sources. * Leverage Power Automate to streamline and automate business processes, integrating with Dynamics 365 and other platforms to ensure efficient data flows and task automation. * Ensure all developed solutions have an intuitive user interface (UI) and are optimised for mobile and desktop experiences. Training and Documentation: * Create comprehensive documentation for developed solutions and processes. * Conduct training sessions for end\-users to ensure effective utilization of applications and tools. Collaboration and Problem Solving: * Work closely with IT and business teams to identify areas for improvement and drive technological solutions. * Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to MS Dynamics, SharePoint, and Power Applications in a timely manner. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field. * Proven experience with MS Dynamics 365, SharePoint Online, and Power Applications. * Experience of Model\-Driven Applications and their implementation within business environments. * Proficiency in Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, and Azure services. * Familiarity with JavaScript, HTML, CSS, and other web technologies is a plus. * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and in a team\-based environment. * Relevant Microsoft certifications desirable (e.g., Dynamics 365, Power Platform). * Experience with Azure DevOps or similar project management tools. * Knowledge of data integration techniques and APIs. * Understanding of information technology fundamental tools and concepts (SDLC) * Strong knowledge of programming languages such as Java or Python. * Experience with database management systems. * Ability to analyze and create tests and develop specifications and requirements for end users to follow. * Ensure all solutions comply with security best practices and relevant regulations (e.g., GDPR) when handling sensitive business data. **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our Company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs.

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included.
