title: Graduate Recruitment Consultant
description: **Graduate Recruitment Consultant– Uncapped Commission!** **Location:** South County Dublin (Sandyford) **Earnings:** €40k\-€60k\+ (Salary \+ Uncapped Commission) GPC is a specialist recruitment consultancy in Dublin with 25\+ years’ experience in Ireland, the UK and internationally. We’re hiring a **Graduate Recruitment Consultant** to join our dynamic team in Sandyford. This role suits an ambitious, confident person who thrives in a fast\-paced, target\-driven environment. It would particularly suit someone coming from a people\-focused background ( **Hospitality, Education, Sales, Recruitment Coordination** ) who is looking to build a long\-term career in recruitment. **Role:** * The successful **Recruitment Consultant** will manage the full 360 recruitment process. * Business development with new and existing clients * Manage your own client database * Source and interview candidates * Creating job specifications for client’s roles. * Receive full training and mentoring from senior consultants \& the MD **Requirements:** * Degree preferred (or equivalent experience) * Minimum 1 year’s experience in an office or recruitment environment * **Background in a people facing role such as Sales, Recruitment Coordination, Hospitality, Education highly desirable.** * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, both written and verbal * Confident speaking on the phone and comfortable with cold calling * Organised, motivated, and results\-driven with strong attention to detail * Ability to work with urgency in a fast\-moving environment **Salary \& Benefits:** * **Basic \+ uncapped commission (OTE €40k–€60k\+)** * Fast\-track career opportunities and hands on mentorship * 23 days leave (25 after 2 years) * Pension and mobile phone * Annual performance bonus * Collaborative, supportive team with regular social events * Monthly, Quarterly \& Annual targets with rewards If you are looking for a challenging and rewarding career as Recruitment Consultant, please send your CV to Caroline

title: Graduate Training Programme - Hotel Management
description: **Overview:** The MHL Hotel Collection is a collection of unique and individual hotels in key city and resort locations throughout Ireland. We are seeking enthusiastic and driven recent graduates to join our hotel teams. The graduate program is designed to provide you with a comprehensive introduction to hospitality and our group through a structured rotational experience. Upon completion of our programme, it is expected that you will be equipped to pursue a management position within the group. **What to expect:** Over an 18month period you will build relationships with colleagues to support your professional development. There will be rotation across not only departments but also within our 13 hotels to enable you to enhance your understanding of the group, and to support your own leadership journey. You will gain invaluable hands\-on experience within the departments, work alongside experienced managers and be assigned work projects that demonstrate your problem solving and analytical abilities. You will benefit from the guidance of a mentor who is assigned to you at the start of the programme, there will be regular check in's and peer meetings, and both internal and external training will be provided. **What we are looking for:** * Due to graduate or recently graduated with a relevant degree (Hospitality or Tourism \- however if you do not have a degree in these fields, but have experience in hospitality, we would still love to hear from you). * Excellent communication, teamwork and interpersonal skills * Ability to adapt to a fast paced environment * Passion for hospitality, guest satisfaction * Willingness to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends * Ideally open to travel within Ireland for the hotel rotations **What we offer:** * A competitive graduate salary * Fast track career progression * Employee Assistance Programme * Meals on duty * Employee appreciation and wellbeing week * Employee discounts * Plus more **The programme will commence in September 2026** This is an excellent opportunity for recent graduates to kickstart their careers and develop a strong foundation within the MHL Hotel Collection *Due to the high volume of applications received, we regret that we are only able to contact candidates whose experience and profile closely match the requirements of the role. We sincerely thank all applicants for their interest and for taking the time to apply.* Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent, Graduate Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Growth Operations Specialist
description: **About Us** How did Web Summit become, in the words of Forbes, “the best tech conference on the planet”? Meaningful connections. Our tech events are unmissable because we make it easier for the right people to meet and connect. Everyone at Web Summit works towards this goal. And we’re just getting started. We’re always looking to build on the impact we have already made at Web Summit. In the coming years we’ll take Web Summit to new markets, promoting global connectivity, highlighting important issues and connecting global leaders – all while making a positive impact on the environment and communities we encounter. To build a better company, we have to better ourselves. We do that by finding the most ambitious people to work with us. **About The Team** The Growth team is at the intersection of marketing, operations and data science. We sit in Marketing \& Analytics, and work with every team in the company to help them achieve their objectives. The end goal of everything we do is to help the company and all the events we run grow at an accelerated rate using a data\-driven, technically\-orientated approach. Our north star is growing numbers and improving processes all across the company. **What You'll Do** * Analyse \& Advise Identify inefficiencies across our GTM and marketing ops workflows and recommend data\-driven improvements * Build \& Automate Develop lightweight and heavy automations and integrations using Python, APIs, and automation tools (Matomo, Zapier, Pipedream, etc) to reduce manual effort and improve data flows * Own CRM Quality Work within Salesforce to manage data integrity, build workflows, and support the broader revenue and marketing teams * Bridge the Gap Act as the connective tissue between technical engineers and non\-technical marketing or commercial stakeholders, confidently translating in both directions * Leverage AI Tooling Use LLM\-powered tools (including Claude Code and agentic workflows) to accelerate analysis, automate tasks, and explore new capabilities * Evaluate \& Adopt Continuously assess new tools and technologies, with a genuine appetite for improving our tech stack. * Collaborate \& Communicate Contribute actively in cross\-functional projects, producing clear documentation, presentations, and recommendations for varied audiences **What We're Looking For** **Technical Skills** * Proficiency in Python for data manipulation, scripting, and light automation * Strong SQL skills for querying and analysing data across relational databases * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce or equivalent CRM platforms * Comfortable working with REST APIs (authentication, pagination, data ingestion) * Experience with automation platforms such as Matomo, Zapier, Pipedream, etc * Exposure to LLMs and AI tooling , prompt engineering, agentic workflows, or tools like Claude Code is a strong plus **Soft Skills \& Mindset** * Excellent verbal and written communication skills , able to adapt your style for technical and non\-technical audiences alike * Strong interest in marketing, business, or partnerships * Demonstrated ability to learn quickly and adapt in a fast\-moving environment * Curious and self\-directed , you actively seek out better ways of doing things * Comfortable with ambiguity and able to define structure where little exists * A natural collaborator who thrives in cross\-functional team environments * Detail\-oriented without losing sight of the bigger commercial picture **Skills We Like To See** * 2\+ years in a Business Analyst, Marketing Operations, Revenue Operations, or similar hybrid technical/business role * Experience working within or alongside a marketing, growth, or GTM function is a significant advantage, candidates who have operated inside a commercial or go\-to\-market team will ramp up faster and add immediate value **Benefits And Perks** * Subsidised healthcare, dental, employee assistance programme (EAP) and more. * Employer pension contribution up to 6% for Irish\-based employees * A dedicated in\-house L\&D department, with access to workshops, online learning and resources to help you excel in your career development. * Company MacBook, generous annual leave and flexible working arrangements. * Our very own Tramway Team, which drives community and morale\-boosting events for employees to participate in. * Annual company get\-togethers, charity days, and monthly wellbeing talks. * Wellness subsidy issued to all employees of €1000 per year **At Web Summit, we embrace diversity and inclusion. We want talent from all walks of life to help us on our journey to digitise the conference sector. Hiring decisions are made purely on experience, skills and the needs of the business.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: HR DATA ANALYST
description: Job Title **HR Data Analyst** Location **Remote – Ireland** Contract Type **6 Month Contract** Role Overview I’m currently partnering with a fast\-paced, international technology organisation operating within the blockchain space to appoint a **HR Data Analyst** on an initial **6\-month contract** basis. This is a fully remote role based in Ireland and would suit someone who enjoys working with HR data, systems, compliance, accuracy and process improvement. The organisation is currently going through significant HR systems and data transformation work, so they are looking for someone highly organised, analytical and naturally detail\-oriented who can support data integrity across HR, payroll and finance\-related processes. This is not a traditional people\-focused HR role. It is best suited to someone who enjoys structure, logic, systems, reporting, compliance and ensuring data is accurate and reliable. The Opportunity As HR Data Analyst, you will work closely with HR, payroll, finance and systems stakeholders to support a number of key global HR data and compliance initiatives. You will be involved in HRIS and payroll data migration, data cleansing, validation, reporting, audit support and process improvement. The environment is fast\-moving and evolving, so this role would suit someone adaptable, resilient and comfortable working through ambiguity while maintaining a high level of accuracy. Key Responsibilities * Manage, maintain and validate HR data across multiple systems. * Support the transition from BambooHR and Gusto into Rippling. * Conduct regular HR data audits and identify inconsistencies, gaps or risks. * Support HRIS and payroll data migration projects. * Validate HR, payroll and finance\-related data to ensure accuracy and compliance. * Work with HR, payroll, finance and systems teams to improve workflows and system connectivity. * Support access management, permissions and custom field configuration within HR systems. * Assist with payroll and accounts payable\-related administration and reconciliation work. * Support reporting, budgeting and compliance tracking activities. * Help improve HR data processes, documentation standards and audit trails. * Proactively identify opportunities to make HR data processes more scalable, accurate and reliable. Requirements * 2–4 years’ experience in a HR data, HR operations, HRIS, payroll, compliance or data\-focused role. * Strong analytical and numerical ability. * Experience working with HR systems, payroll data or finance\-related processes. * Previous exposure to payroll and/or accounts payable would be highly advantageous. * Strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy. * Ability to independently check, validate and sense\-check data. * Comfortable working with evolving systems, processes and transformation projects. * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. * Excellent communication skills and ability to work across HR, payroll and finance teams. * Experience with Rippling, BambooHR or Gusto would be advantageous. * Must be based in Ireland. * Fluent English essential. The Ideal Person This role would suit someone who: * Enjoys working with data, systems and process improvement. * Has a logical, structured and analytical approach. * Takes ownership and follows tasks through to completion. * Is compliance\-focused and detail\-oriented. * Enjoys problem solving and improving processes. * Can work well in a fast\-paced, scaling environment. * Is proactive, dependable and comfortable working independently. Additional Information * **Job Title:** HR Data Analyst * **Location:** Remote, Ireland * **Contract:** Initial 6\-month contract * **Start Date:** Immediate availability or short notice preferred * **Industry:** International technology / blockchain * **Working Model:** Fully remote

title: Human Resources Business Partner
description: **Role Overview** Our client, a maturing and innovative biotech company, with their headquarters in Dublin, is seeking an HR Business Partner to work closely with the Chief People Officer (CPO) in shaping and delivering a high\-impact people strategy. This is a broad, hands\-on HRBP role operating in a hybrid environment, supporting a growing, science\-led organisation through its next phase of scale. The position will span strategic business partnering alongside operational HR delivery, including recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding. The successful candidate will be commercially aware, pragmatic, and comfortable balancing strategic input with day\-to\-day execution in a fast\-paced, evolving environment. **Key Responsibilities** **Business Partnering \& People Strategy** * Partner closely with the CPO and senior leaders to align people strategy with business objectives. * Provide coaching and support to managers on performance, engagement, and team effectiveness. * Support organisational design, workforce planning, and scaling initiatives. * Contribute to building a high\-performance, values\-driven culture. **Talent Acquisition \& Workforce Growth** * Lead and support end\-to\-end recruitment processes across technical and corporate roles. * Work with hiring managers to define role requirements, sourcing strategies, and interview processes. * Enhance employer branding and ensure a positive candidate experience. **Onboarding \& Employee Experience** * Design and deliver effective onboarding processes to ensure successful employee integration. * Partner with managers to ensure clear role expectations and early performance alignment. * Drive initiatives to improve employee engagement and retention. **Employee Relations \& HR Operations** * Act as a trusted advisor on employee relations matters, ensuring fair and consistent outcomes. * Support performance management processes, including reviews, development planning, and performance improvement cases. * Ensure HR policies and procedures are compliant with Irish employment legislation and best practice. **Offboarding \& Lifecycle Management** * Manage structured offboarding processes, including exit interviews and feedback analysis. * Provide insight into attrition trends and recommend retention strategies. * Ensure compliance and positive employer brand is maintained through employee departures. **Process Improvement \& HR Projects** * Support the CPO in implementing scalable HR processes and systems. * Contribute to HR analytics, reporting, and insights to inform decision\-making. * Participate in key HR initiatives such as performance frameworks, DEI, and leadership development. **Candidate Profile** **Experience \& Qualifications** * Proven experience in an HR Business Partner or Senior HR Generalist role, ideally within biotech, life sciences, tech, or a scaling organisation. * Strong working knowledge of Irish employment law and HR best practices. * Experience supporting recruitment and talent lifecycle activities end\-to\-end. * Involvement in a fast\-paced, growth\-oriented or changing environment. * Relevant HR qualification (CIPD or equivalent) preferred. **Key Characteristics** * Strong interpersonal and influencing skills, with the ability to build credibility at all levels. * Commercially aware with a pragmatic, solutions\-focused approach. * Comfortable operating both strategically and operationally. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail. * Proactive, adaptable, and resilient in a dynamic environment. * Collaborative mindset with a strong sense of ownership. **Why Apply?** * Join a leading\-edge biotech company at an exciting stage of growth and maturity * Work directly with a CPO and leadership team to shape the people agenda * Broad, impactful role with exposure to both strategy and execution * Competitive compensation and long\-term career development potential

title: IT Business Analyst – Systems/Applications
description: We are seeking a Technical Business Analyst that will support the delivery, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the client’s business systems and applications. Thie ideal candidate will have experince working in ***systems delivery, change, or project environments.*** The successful candidate will work closely with internal teams, external partners, and the wider dealer network to ensure high quality system performance and project delivery. **Key Responsibilities** **Systems and Application** * Support integrations, API based services, and cloud hosted applications * Work with third party development partners on requirements, documentation, testing, and release preparation * Assist with version control, code reviews, and system documentation * Develop internal tools or utilities to improve operational efficiency **Helpdesk Escalations and Technical Support** * Serve as an escalation point for complex systems, software, and workflow issues * Provide hands on troubleshooting and root cause analysis for application related incidents * Support the helpdesk team during peak periods and critical incidents * Collaborate with developers and vendors to resolve defects and system errors * Assist internal and external users during planned updates or system rollouts **Project Delivery and Governance** * Deliver technology and software projects using structured project management approaches * Gather requirements, define project scope, and document business processes * Coordinate with internal stakeholders, external suppliers, and business teams * Monitor project risks, progress, and communication activities * Support change management and adoption of new features or systems **Software Quality Assurance** * Carry out functional, regression, and integration testing * Log issues with clarity, prioritise them appropriately, and follow through to closure * Support user acceptance testing and gather feedback from business users * Maintain test plans, acceptance criteria, and quality documentation **Artificial Intelligence and Automation** * Use AI tools to support documentation, analysis, troubleshooting, and development tasks * Identify opportunities for AI driven automation within business systems and support processes * Assist staff in the safe and effective use of AI technologies * Contribute to AI related initiatives across internal systems and operational workflows **Required Skills And Experience** * A relevant third level degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field. * Two to five years’ experience as a Business Analyst in a software, systems, or application focused IT role * Strong troubleshooting and problem\-solving ability * Experience with system integrations and APIs * Understanding of the software development lifecycle and release management * Experience supporting or maintaining business applications * Strong communication skills and ability to work with both technical and non\-technical users * Ability to manage workload across development, support, and project activities * Experience working with third party development partners * Strong team player **Preferred Qualifications And Experience** * PRINCE2, Agile, Scrum, or similar project management certification * Experience with modern CRM Systems (Salesforce, MS Dynamics, others) * Experience with scripting, automation, data workflows, and minor feature enhancements * Practical experience using AI tools for automation, analysis, or workflow improvement Apply Now

title: IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** We are seeking a skilled and proactive IT Operations Engineer to join our team. In this critical role, you will be instrumental in delivering IT support to Distilled’s Employees enabling a seamless hybrid working environment and ensuring everyone has the tools and support needed to do their best work, while maintaining the smooth operation and continuous improvement of our internal IT systems. You'll leverage your expertise to resolve complex technical challenges, contribute to strategic IT initiatives and work with a diverse range of cutting\-edge technologies that drive our company's success. This is an exciting opportunity to deepen your technical knowledge, enhance our operational efficiency, and play a key role in scaling our IT environment. **Location:** This role requires working from our Dublin office 4 days per week. **What will I be doing** As an IT Operations Engineer, you, along with the existing ITOps team, will be responsible for a wide range of technical operations, ensuring the delivery of dependable and resilient IT services. The ideal candidate can balance reactive support—managing urgent incoming issues—with proactive work such as scheduled maintenance and strategic improvements, while consistently looking for opportunities to optimize systems \& processes and to ensure that our employees have the tools to do their best work. * **IT Support \& Service Delivery:** Deliver comprehensive remote and onsite IT support through the ticketing system for approximately 250 employees across multiple locations and countries, with a strong focus on providing an excellent service experience for all users. Responsibilities include troubleshooting hardware, software, and system issues, managing equipment lifecycle and procurement, and overseeing the user lifecycle, including onboarding and offboarding. The role also requires a proactive approach to anticipating user needs and continuously improving the support experience. * **Infrastructure Administration:** Administer, monitor and optimize critical IT infrastructure, including networking, firewalls, video conferencing and AV systems, ensuring performance, security and high availability. Manage and secure remote devices via Jamf, Intune and Google Workspace * **Identity \& Access Management:** Manage user accounts and permissions within Okta/Active Directory, encompassing security policy and MFA administration. Optimise the IT aspects of employee onboarding and offboarding processes. * **Operational Excellence \& Documentation:** Drive continuous improvement initiatives for IT operations. Create and maintain comprehensive IT documentation. Co\-manage IT asset inventory and refine escalation processes. * **Project Contribution \& Event Support:** Contribute to the planning and implementation phases of IT projects with a focus on innovation, automation and security. Provide technical support for company\-wide initiatives and events. **What skills and experience do I need** * At least 3 years experience in an IT Operations or System Administration role providing hands on support for employees. * Extensive enterprise experience administering and troubleshooting macOS, Windows, and mobile devices, with strong expertise in MDM solutions including Intune, Jamf and Google Workspace. * Proven expertise in administering and supporting Google Workspace or Office 365\. * Hands\-on experience with administering Cloud Computing platforms (AWS/Azure/ Google Cloud), including IaaS and PaaS components. * Experience in managing and troubleshooting enterprise\-level Video Conferencing systems. * Solid understanding and practical experience with Networking concepts (VPNs, Firewalls, Wi\-Fi, VLANs, routing, switching). * Experience with Identity Management platforms (e.g., Okta, Azure AD Identity Protection, SSO integration). * GDPR and information security focused. * A proactive, adaptable professional with outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, as well as a technical and problem\-solving mindset. **Advantage if you have:** * Experience with scripting languages for automation and system administration. * Familiarity with IT security best practices and incident response. * Industry recognised credentials: Microsoft Azure/Endpoint/SecOps, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Okta Professional/Administrator, Jamf 100/170/300 , Associate Google Workspace Administrator, Comptia Security\+, ITIL Foundation. * Experience in upcoming AI trends in predictive security, agentic AI for automation and specialized IT service management (ITSM) to handle AI\-driven threats **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together** . **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen** . We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: IT Support Engineer - Level 2
description: **PFH Technology are currently looking for an experienced L2 IT Support Engineer to join our team in Dublin.** **Objective:** Provide hands on end user support, administration, installation and issue resolution onsite for our client. **Contract:** 12\-month contract (with expected extensions) **Location** : Fully onsite, Dublin 1 **Prerequisites:** ITIL V4, Microsoft Certifications including MD 102 **Skills/Requirements** **1\. Technical Skills** * **Operating Systems:** Advanced knowledge of various operating systems like Windows, macOS, and Linux. * Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting OS\-related issues. * Knowledge of Active Directory (AD), Group Policies, and domain\-related issues (for Windows environments). * **Network Troubleshooting:** * In\-depth understanding of networking concepts such as DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, and firewalls. * Diagnosing and resolving issues related to routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. * Monitoring and maintaining network performance. * **Hardware and Software Troubleshooting:** * Ability to diagnose hardware failures (laptops, desktops, servers). * Knowledge of software deployment, updates, and patch management. * **Virtualization:** Familiarity with virtualization platforms like VMware, Hyper\-V, or VirtualBox. * **Cloud Technologies:** Understanding cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and managing cloud services like virtual machines, storage, and cloud backups. * **Scripting and Automation:** Basic scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Bash, Python) for automating routine tasks and troubleshooting complex issues. **2\. Advanced Troubleshooting and Problem Solving** * **Root Cause Analysis:** Ability to perform deep dives into issues to identify root causes rather than simply resolving symptoms. * **Log Analysis:** Reading and interpreting system and network logs to diagnose and resolve technical issues. * **System Performance Tuning:** Diagnosing performance bottlenecks in hardware, applications, and networks, and suggesting improvements. * **Incident Management:** Following standard ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) practices for incident escalation, documentation, and resolution. **3\. Communication Skills** * **Customer Service:** Communicating complex technical information in a user\-friendly way to non\-technical staff or clients. * **Collaboration:** Working effectively with Level 1 (frontline) support teams, and escalating issues to Level 3 or specialized support teams when necessary. * **Documentation:** Creating and updating technical documentation, including knowledge base articles and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * **Ticket Management:** Using ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk) to manage and resolve technical support requests. **4\. Security Awareness** * **Cybersecurity Knowledge:** Awareness of security best practices, threat mitigation, and basic concepts like encryption, VPNs, multi\-factor authentication (MFA), and firewalls. * **Endpoint Security:** Ensuring that workstations, laptops, and mobile devices are secured against potential threats. * **Backup and Disaster Recovery:** Familiarity with backup solutions, disaster recovery planning, and ensuring data integrity. **5\. Database Management** * **Basic SQL Knowledge:** Understanding of databases and SQL queries for troubleshooting database\-related issues or working with database administrators (DBAs). * **Backup and Restore:** Ability to perform basic database backups and restore operations in case of failure. **6\. Project Support** * **Support for IT Projects:** Participating in IT projects such as software rollouts, infrastructure upgrades, or migrations. * **Change Management:** Understanding change control processes and ensuring that system changes are well\-documented and implemented according to organizational policies. **7\. Remote Support Tools** * **Remote Troubleshooting:** Proficiency with tools like TeamViewer, AnyDesk, or Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for assisting users remotely. * **Monitoring Tools:** Familiarity with network and system monitoring tools (e.g., Nagios, SolarWinds, PRTG) to proactively identify issues. **8\. Time Management and Prioritization** * **Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while ensuring timely resolution. **9\. Vendor and Third\-Party Support** * **Vendor Management:** Liaising with third\-party vendors for escalated technical issues related to specific hardware or software products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. **10\. Certifications:** **Essential** * **Microsoft Certifications:** (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified). * **ITIL Foundation V4:** For understanding IT service management (ITSM) processes. **Optional but Beneficial** * **CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+** for networking and security fundamentals. * **Cisco Certifications:** (e.g., CCNA for networking fundamentals). **Education** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway.

title: Implementation Consultant – Fintech
description: **Implementation Consultant – Fintech** This is an excellent opportunity to join a well\-established, high\-performing software business operating within the financial technology space. The company develops enterprise\-grade platforms used by global clients to manage complex financial operations and reporting processes. The business continues to invest in modern technologies, automation, and AI\-driven solutions to improve efficiency and deliver value to clients. **About the role** We are looking for an **Implementation Consultant** who combines strong technical capability with client\-facing confidence. This role suits someone who enjoys solving complex problems, working with data, and guiding customers through system implementations. You’ll likely have: * **2\-5 years’ experience** in a technical, consulting, or fintech\-related role * Exposure to **financial services, fund accounting, or investment operations** (highly beneficial) * Strong **data handling skills** , with experience in **SQL and/or advanced Excel** * A genuine interest in **AI, automation, and improving operational efficiency** * The ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * A proactive mindset with strong problem\-solving and organisational skills **Responsibilities** In this position, you’ll work closely with clients and internal teams to deliver and support enterprise software solutions. Your day\-to\-day will include: * Gathering and understanding client requirements and business processes * Configuring and tailoring software solutions to meet customer needs * Supporting system implementations, testing phases, and go\-live activities * Writing and executing test cases and validation processes * Collaborating with development teams to enhance product functionality * Managing and resolving client queries, issues, and change requests * Troubleshooting problems and delivering clear, effective solutions * Supporting integrations, data workflows, and system configurations * Leveraging AI tools and automation to improve efficiency and delivery **Nice to Have / Bonus Skills** While not essential, the following would strengthen your application: * Knowledge of investment products, fund administration, or financial reporting * Experience with SQL, database queries, or data analysis tools * Familiarity with AI tools or modern development assistants (e.g. Claude, Copilot, automation tools) * Exposure to software testing frameworks or development tools * Experience working with support systems or ticketing platforms * Understanding of cloud platforms or modern software delivery environments **Why Join** * Excellent salary package with performance incentives * Great benefits including pension, healthcare, and wellness support * Hybrid working model * Very strong team environment with experienced leadership * Low attrition and long\-tenured team * Clear opportunities for career progression and skill development * Exposure to modern technologies, AI tools, and global clients

title: Information Technology Service Engineer
description: Sysco are currently recruiting for a **onsite Services Engineer** to join the **Dublin** , Ireland Technology team reporting to the Services Engineer Manager. This position will be responsible for delivering Sysco technology services support located at sites within their designated region. You will be a resourceful, reliable and trustworthy person who’s accurate with numbers and able to use mathematical formulas within spreadsheets with excellent communication skills, (both written and verbal), organisational, presentation and problem\-solving skills.

title: Information Technology Support Analyst

If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. Visit our website for more information or explore additional opportunities

title: Information Technology Support Engineer
description: Excellent job opportunity for an IT Support Engineer with a dynamic and innovative company provide EPOS, dispensary and 3rd party ordering systems to clients in order to improve business model and optimise operations with increased efficiency and speed of delivery for customers. This role is a hybrid work model with office based in Dublin. **Key responsibilities** * Providing product support to customers across Ireland. * Liaising staff out in the field to address client product issues or EPOS issues in\-store. * To cross train into other areas of customer service as required * Foster excellent relations with all customers and to all new and existing customers. * Log and manage all customer queries through the Magna CRM system (based on Microsoft Dynamics365\). * Setting up new customers on product EPOS and ordering systems. * Assist with data migrations from various dispensary software systems, which may be required outside of typical business hours from time to time. **Work activities include:** * Logging incoming customer technical issues, take ownership to root causing the problem and work on resolving each issue. * Communicating by phone with customers on technical issues and supporting them to resolve all issues. * Communicate with third party technical vendors for third level support * Occasional on\-site visits to customers to resolve problems that could not be addressed over the phone. * Site visits to support with the installation of equipment and software prior to training day. title: Information Technology Support Engineer

title: Java Developer
description: **About the role:** You'll be working on the backend of systems that move money; payment processing engines, merchant integration services, and the microservices that orchestrate transactions across multiple payment rails. With over 20 years of experience in online payments, we have built a highly secure technology platform and a growing global network of financial and banking partners. As we continue to expand across multiple global verticals, we foster a collaborative, inclusive work environment while maintaining a start\-up culture that values flexibility, autonomy, and innovation across all of our offices in Dublin, Malta, and Belfast. **Responsibilities** : * Develop new in\-house products * Translate application storyboards and use cases into feasible functional applications * Design, build, and maintain efficient, reusable, and reliable Java code * Write clean code supported with unit testing modules * Ensure the best possible performance, quality, scalability and responsiveness of the applications * Document features and share knowledge within the team * Collaborate with other team members and stakeholders * Help maintain code quality, organization, and automatization including undertaking peer code reviews * Perform integrations with 3rd party applications **Qualifications** : * Minimum 3\+ years of JAVA enterprise software experience * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science or related field * Self\-Motivated Individual with excellent interpersonal and communication skills having the ability to work in a team and on own initiative * Experience with JAVA Spring Boot, Caching methodologies and Database Design would be considered as a great plus * Experience in working within a microservice architectural approach * Ability to write clean code with an eye for performance and scalability * Strong analytical and problem solving skills * Familiar with various software design principles and architectural patterns behind a scalable application * Creating database schemas that represent and support business processes * Desirable to have experience with RabbitMQ \& Docker * Focused on driving standards in AI assisted development * Experience in financial software development will be considered an asset.

title: Junior Business Analyst
description: **GET TO KNOW US** At Brown Thomas Arnotts, we are Reinventing Retail. Our purpose is to enrich our customer’s lives, which we achieve by living our values – Going Above and Beyond, Driving Creativity and Innovation, and Doing the Right Thing. We are one business with two iconic brands, bringing exciting experience to life through our digital and physical destinations. Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **KNOW THE ROLE** The Junior Business Analyst will work as part of the Digital Products team, and support the Senior Product Owner in delivering the digital roadmap across web and mobile app, taking ownership of the day\-to\-day execution of work through Jira to support the Senior PO to focus on backlog strategy, stakeholder management, and solution design. Key Responsibilities: * Translate defined requirements into well\-structured Jira tickets (user stories, acceptance criteria, technical notes) for both web and app development tracks * Maintain Jira hygiene: grooming, prioritisation support, sprint preparation, and reporting * Liaise with developers and QA to clarify requirements, unblock queries, and support delivery through the sprint cycle * Support release activities including release notes, regression coordination, and post\-release validation * Document processes, decisions, and platform behaviours to build a reusable internal knowledge base * Pick up smaller pieces of analysis and discovery work under the Senior PO's direction, growing into broader product responsibilities over time **KNOW WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR** * 1–2 years' experience in a BA, Associate PO, or similar delivery role, ideally in e\-commerce, retail, or digital product * Comfortable working in Jira and Confluence; familiarity with Agile/Scrum ceremonies * Comfortable analysing API contracts — endpoints, request/response parameters, attributes, and payload structures — to support a headless architecture across web and app * Strong written communication — able to write clear, unambiguous tickets * Detail\-oriented, organised, and comfortable managing multiple workstreams in parallel * Curious about technology and willing to develop a working understanding of our platform (Salesforce Commerce Cloud) and mobile app stack * Comfortable using AI tools (e.g. Claude) to accelerate day\-to\-day work — drafting tickets, summarising requirements, interrogating documentation, and exploring solution options — with the judgement to validate outputs before they're actioned * A collaborative working style — comfortable interfacing with developers, QA, and business stakeholders * Exposure to SFCC or a comparable enterprise e\-commerce platform * Experience working across both web and native mobile app delivery **KNOW WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU** We believe that every team member should have the opportunity to curate a meaningful career that enhances professional and personal development. That is why we foster a culture of belonging and inclusiveness where your voice is valuable. A great customer experience starts with a great people experience, and we’re proud to have been recognised by multiple industry bodies for our progressive workplace culture. As a Brown Thomas Arnotts team member, you will benefit from: * Competitive Salary * Contributory Pension Scheme * Competitive annual leave entitlement * Team Member discount across the world’s most iconic brands * Recognition Days for Volunteering \& Wellbeing * Wellbeing Initiatives * Extensive Learning \& Development Offerings * Leading Talent Programmes * Flexible Working Model * Additional Benefits **KNOW HOW WE WORK** **Experience is our business:** Our customer engagement sets the bar in service and experiential retail, giving our customers something to love every time. **We Are More Than Shops:** We create enjoyable places, to excite, where people socialise, discover, learn and are inspired, and where local communities are enriched. **We are Curators:** Our unique range of product and services are key to the sense of excitement that Brown Thomas Arnotts is renowned for, whether for a special day or every day, things that make our customer’s lives better. **We Care for Our People:** We believe a great customer experience starts with a great people experience. That is why we promote a culture where talent is nurtured and where creativity and learning are valued. **We Succeed by Working Sustainably:** We are community\-focused and our aim is to cultivate retail environments that are conscious, considerate and commercial. Brown Thomas Arnotts is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where culture and creativity come together. Our future success depends on the perspectives and contributions of all team members – their diverse backgrounds, abilities and experiences make our business stronger. We don’t discriminate on the basis of gender, marital status, family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, race, religion, and / or membership of the Travelling community. We aim to ensure the candidate experience is accessible to all and every new team member who joins Brown Thomas Arnotts can expect to become part of a progressive community where different backgrounds, views \& personalities aren’t just welcomed – they’re positively nurtured. After all, we want you to bring your whole self to work. Please contact us at careers@brownthomas.ie to request accommodation.

title: Lecturer
company: Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland (RCSI)
description: **Job Title:** Lecturer in Dental Education and Primary Care Dentistry **Reporting To:** Accountable to Professor Albert Leung, Head of the School of Dentistry **Location:** RCSI, Sandyford Dental Education Centre (DEC), other DECs as they are developed, RCSI St Stephen’s Green Campus and all other RCSI owned or commissioned teaching, research and clinical facilities. **School (or department):** School of Dentistry **Contract type/duration:** Permanent, full time (1FTE): preferable. *RCSI may consider part time options of no less than 0\.8 FTE, subject to the successful candidate and RCSI needs.* **Closing date for applications:** 24th June 2026 Specific Duties of the Roles: **Programme Delivery and Development:** RCSI School of Dentistry operates a community\-based BDS curriculum, which aligns with the relevant dentistry regulatory requirements for undergraduate teaching in Ireland. The School of Dentistry also currently offers Level 10 Programmes (PhD and Professional Doctorates), and more academic programmes will continue to be developed in due course. • You will be responsible for procuring and implementing all aspects of Dental Education and Primary Care Dentistry, and also within your area(s) of specialist/special interests in clinical dentistry and dental education. • You will be a Leader in delivering all aspects (Clinical, Service, Teaching and Research) Dental Education and Primary Care Dentistry. • You will be working collaboratively with the team to contribute to and participate in the continuous development and delivery of the School’s level 8 (and where appropriate level 9 and level 10 as well, including academic supervision) educational programme. • You will contribute to the development and enhancement of the School of Dentistry Curricula, ensuring that the applications to clinical practice contents are current, relevant, and aligned with advancements in the practice of dentistry. **Programme Organisation:** You will be one of the leaders and be working with your peers to advance the programme of work to enable implementation of this ambitious project, including supporting; • The development of teaching and assessment schedules based on the proposed programme whilst utilizing available resources as efficiently as possible. • The continuous implementation and set up of Dental Education Centres (DECs) – a key facility for the delivery of community\-based dentistry model, and positive learning experiences for students. • The technology platform development to support the delivery of the dentistry programme in close collaboration with IT colleagues. • The development of the Marks and Standards documentation. • The development of and coordination of in\-module and end of module assessments as required. • The quality assurance/quality improvement and accreditation of existing modules. Where applicable support External Examiner / Accreditation audits or activities. • To act as module lead, or year lead, as requested • To act as module co\-ordinator or year co\-ordinator, as requested • To act as an Academic Personal Tutor as part of Professionalism module. • To be involved in Enquiry\-Based Learning (EBL) or similar activities, including review of the cases \& development of educational \& assessment materials, and the delivery of appropriate EBL activities. • To be involved in Simulation Dental Learning Environment (SDLE) activities, and lead sessions and assessments as necessary. • Develop relevant Policies, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for the School of Dentistry in accordance with Irish regulations. **Teaching** • Teaching in all aspects of dental education, with an emphasis on primary care dentistry, academic and curriculum development and support, mentoring students, conducting research as appropriate, and collaborating with industry and the academic community. • Prepare and lead the delivery of teaching to students across the dentistry programmes. Ensure that modules being taught are delivered and examined appropriately and effectively and in adherence to the relevant Curriculum Course Book and Marks \& Standards regulations. • Integrate innovative teaching methods, technology, and research findings into the curriculum of the dentistry programmes to enhance students’ learning experience, in accordance with the School of Dentistry teaching and assessment strategies. • Act as liaison between students and staff managing Student communications (Academic Performance; Approach to learning journey; Content sign posting etc.). • In time, the School of Dentistry shall be developing NFQ levels 9 and 10 programmes. As a Lecturer, you will be actively contributing to this drive in dental education and primary care dentistry, from curriculum design, assessment planning, facilities procurements, organisation of clinical and other pedagogic materials and capacity building in teaching, service provision and the relevant research. **Clinical Teaching and Service Provision** • To be actively involved in Clinical Activity, and to lead and co\-ordinate where appropriate, including all aspects of primary care dentistry in any of the Dental Education Centres (including clinics) or other relevant facilities where RCSI operates or uses, including student supervision, assessments and service provision as necessary. • To conduct clinical research where appropriate as part of the research output for this post. • Where appropriate, to co\-ordinate and lead the procurement, integration, and the delivery of dental education particularly in relation to clinical and service provision **Research** • To pursue sustained high quality research activities through original research and scholarship, including other research related contributions such as publications in high impact journals, conference presentations, consultancy projects/advice in dentistry, particularly in the field of dental education and primary care dentistry. • To conduct original research in basic and dental sciences related to the practice of dentistry and in dental education. • To sustain an independent research profile aligned with established priority areas of research within the School and across RCSI. • Where applicable, to collaborate with other researchers, clinicians, and dental healthcare professionals in related fields to advance the understanding of oral health and dental education. • Mentor and supervise undergraduate and postgraduate students where appropriate • Leading sustained impact activities including public engagements, and therein making a significant contribution to the development of appropriate dental education and primary care dentistry policy at a national and international level. The Ideal Candidate: Will have demonstrable evidence of the below: • A PhD in an area relevant to dentistry or dental education or equivalent (for example, Level 10 Professional Doctorate) • Completion of appropriate postgraduate training in the relevant discipline in clinical/general dentistry • Possession of an MFDRCSI, and/or an MGDSRCSI, or an FFDRCSI, or an FGDSRCSI, or a Fellowship from a Royal College of Surgeons in the UK in the same discipline as above (Desirable) • Full registration as a dentist with the Dental Council in Ireland or holds a qualification recognised by the Dental Council in Ireland for registration as a dentist in Ireland AND • Experience of university teaching, particularly in clinical dentistry • Strong educational interest, acumen and skill set, as evidenced by experience in undergraduate and/or postgraduate teaching and assessment in dentistry or relevant health science domains. • An ability to engage undergraduate students through a range of teaching modes. • An ability to continuously adapt and innovate teaching methods and in dental education • Demonstrate an interest in seeking opportunities to support students’ whole development • Strong commitment to own personal and professional development. • Effective communication style appropriate to audience and situation. • Able to build and maintain strong working relationships. • Requires minimum direction to effectively deliver on assigned functions, and demonstrates a positive, student –centred and solution focused approach to addressing challenges and ambiguity. • Has flexibility to work irregular hours on occasion, and for international travel on occasion if required. • Deadline\-oriented while delivering high quality teaching. • An active researcher with research track records particularly in the areas of digital dentistry, prosthodontics and/or periodontics. • A sustained track record of multiple senior authorship of papers in high impact peer\-reviewed journals and of securing competitive grant funding as PI, industrial sponsorships and other external funding and/or patents in the areas of digital dentistry, diagnostics, prosthodontics. • Qualification in teaching and learning in higher education. *We are all too aware that imposter syndrome and the confidence gap can sometimes stop fantastic candidates putting themselves forward, so please do apply — we would love to hear from you.* **Application Process:** Please apply online through the RCSI careers portal on the closing date with your CV and cover letter.

title: Legal Secretary

This role is 3 days in office, 2 from home. Salary range dependent on experience but potential for 45\-50k. **A little bit about the firm:** * A full service law firm. * A head count of 100\+ * A Firm Currently attracting progressively more and more much sought after clientele and are continuously growing their pool of clients at the moment, with significant names in both the private and public sector. **A Little bit about you:** * You are not doing FE1s nor have ambitions to become a solicitor in the foreseeable future. * 1\+ years secretarial experience in a commercial litigation department * Strong communication skills and good telephone manner * Experience managing a portfolio of senior stakeholders within the legal field. * MS Office skills are essential. * Meticulous attention to detail. * Be able to work on own initiative and as part of a team **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Provide comprehensive administrative support including document drafting, diary management, and general legal administration (scanning, printing, photocopying). * Manage the end\-to\-end file lifecycle using the firm’s legal case management system. * Prepare materials and information for internal and external meetings. * Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to understand and respond to their requirements. * Maintain discretion and confidentiality when handling sensitive information. * Ensure accuracy and speed in document production, coordinating with document specialists when required. * Manage travel arrangements and detailed itineraries. * Process time records and expense reports. * Greet and liaise with clients and business visitors in a professional manner We at Search Talent are encouraging anyone who even wants to consider a fantastic and hybrid option for them in the legal secretarial market, and with a background as a legal secretary in this area, to come into contact and we can discuss if this is the best option for you before taking a step further. title: Logistics & Execution Traineeship Program
description: **Location:** The work location can be any ADM location in EMEA. **What the job is about** Within the ADM Logistics \& Execution Trainee Program, you will unlock your full potential. At ADM, we need people who are passionate about solving the nutritional challenges of today and tomorrow. People who want to contribute to something greater than themselves, making a lasting impact. Your first year is dedicated to learning the grassroots of logistics, contract execution and transportation through hands\-on, meaningful roles within one of our European locations. In the second year, you with further develop these skills while having more autonomy at another location in Europe. You will follow a learning plan which balances business requirements and personal development. You will be supported by a mentor from the European logistics and execution team. During your traineeship, you will learn about contract law, logistics, contract execution, risk management, transportation, production planning and the financial implications of execution decisions. After the successful completion of the trainee program, you are prepared for a future logistics or execution role in one of our European locations. **What will make you successful** * Masters or bachelor degree, preferably in Logistics, Economics, Business, Maritime Logistics/Transportation or other related field * Interest in logistical processes * Strong drive for success, great analytical skills, self\-motivated and strive for continuous improvement * IT skilled, great team player and communicator, good with numbers, structured, accurate, out of the box thinking, highly stress resilient * Excellent communication skills in English, additional languages are beneficial * Willing to travel and/or to relocate within Europe * A valid EU work permit **What you will get** * A unique experience in the logistics and execution field, at different European locations across ADM business units * Hands\-on exposure and real responsibility for logistical decisions across multiple markets in line with ADM's European asset footprint * Experience in the dynamics of local and global markets * Meaningful projects to contribute to the global logistics challenges of today and tomorrow * A high level of personal responsibility in a high performing work environment * Excellent career opportunities in a world\-leading agriculture company **About ADM** At ADM, we unlock the power of nature to provide access to nutrition worldwide. With industry\-advancing innovations, a complete portfolio of ingredients and solutions to meet any taste, and a commitment to sustainability, we give customers an edge in solving the nutritional challenges of today and tomorrow. We’re a global leader in human and animal nutrition and the world’s premier agricultural origination and processing company. Our breadth, depth, insights, facilities and logistical expertise give us unparalleled capabilities to meet needs for food, beverages, health and wellness, and more. From the seed of the idea to the outcome of the solution, we enrich the quality of life the world over. Learn more at www.adm.com.

title: M365/Cloud Infrastructure Support Engineer
description: ### **Description** Arthur Cox LLP is one of Ireland’s leading law firms. We are an “all\-island” firm with offices in Dublin and Belfast. We also have offices in London, New York, and San Francisco. Our practice encompasses all aspects of corporate and business law. With 600 legal staff and a total headcount of nearly 900, we provide a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks, and financial institutions and established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, we have led the way in the legal field in Ireland. For our clients, we work to deliver the best service and support. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. **Department:** IT Team **Location:** Dublin (Hybrid Model, minimum 3 days onsite) **Reports to:** Head of IT Operations **Contract Type:** Permanent / Full\-time **Background:** Arthur Cox is at the forefront of technology adoption that drive value within the firm, its client's business, and the wider legal industry. IT operations play a central role, connecting digital capabilities to the firm’s objectives and creating a solid foundation for continued success. We are now seeking to hire an experienced M365/Cloud Infrastructure Support Engineer to join our growing IT operations team. **About the Role:** The Microsoft 365 Support Engineer is responsible for the operational support, administration, and continuous improvement of the firm’s M365 environment. This role ensures the delivery of secure, reliable, and high\-quality end\-user services across collaboration, communication, and productivity platforms, aligned with a white\-glove service delivery model. The primary focus is on Microsoft 365 services and platform operations with a strong emphasis on scripting, automation and AI enablement to enhance service delivery, reduce manual effort, and improve operational efficiency. The role also requires strong competency in Azure services supporting identity, integration, and cloud infrastructure alignment. The successful candidate will work collaboratively across multidisciplinary IT teams including cloud, security, service desk, and applications to deliver stable, secure, and scalable infrastructure services. This role also involves direct interaction with managed service providers to support smooth business operations and ongoing digital transformation initiatives.### **Key Responsibilities** **M365 Platform Support \& Administration:*** Provide day\-to\-day support and administration of Microsoft 365 suite: Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, Entra ID, Microsoft Teams, etc. * Troubleshoot complex M365 service issues across identity, mail flow, and collaboration platforms. * Manage user lifecycle processes (joiners, movers, leavers), with a focus on scripted automation. Monitor service health and proactively address performance issues and vendor’s escalations where required. * **Scripting \& Automation:*** Write and maintain advanced scripts to automate repetitive operational processes to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort. * Utilise Microsoft Graph API and Microsoft Graph Explorer to build custom automation, workflow integrations, and diagnostics. Assist with automation, monitoring, and incident response impacting M365 services (e.g. authentication, access, service health). * **Azure Operations:*** Provide operational support for cloud infrastructure across production and non‑production environments. * Monitor platform health, availability, and security, proactively identifying and resolving issues. * Troubleshoot Azure‑related incidents and participate in root‑cause analysis and service improvement activities. * Support hybrid Azure environments, connectivity, and identity integration using Entra ID. **Incident, Problem \& Change Management:*** Manage and resolve incidents within defined SLAs, ensuring high\-quality updates and communication. * Follow ITIL\-driven processes for change, problem, and service performance management. Participate in root\-cause analysis and continuous improvement activities * **Security, Compliance \& Governance:*** Ensure cloud resources comply with security baselines, governance standards, and regulatory requirements. * Contribute to backup, disaster recovery, and business continuity planning for production workloads. * Ensure compliance with organisational, regulatory, and security standards. **Documentation \& Continuous Improvement:*** Contribute to the ongoing evolution of M365 and Azure services. * Identify opportunities to improve resilience, security, and operational efficiency. * Develop and maintain technical documentation, runbooks, and operational standards for cloud platforms. Participate in knowledge sharing and continuous improvement initiatives within the cloud operations team. * **Project Delivery \& Continuous Improvement:*** Support the delivery of infrastructure projects, upgrades, and migrations. * Contribute to infrastructure modernisation initiatives, including cloud adoption/migration * Identify opportunities for automation, optimisation, and simplification. * Develop and maintain technical documentation, support guidelines, and runbooks. ### **Skills Knowledge and Expertise** * Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology or related field AND * 5 years’ experience in cloud or infrastructure support, with strong hands\-on M365 and Azure operations experience. Strong PowerShell scripting and automation experience in enterprise environments. * **Certifications (Highly desirable):*** Microsoft 365 Certified: Associate (Endpoint, Teams, Identify and Access Administrator. * Azure Fundamentals or above. * ITIL Foundation or similar service management awareness. * CISSP or similar security certification a plus. **Technical skills:*** Strong hands\-on experience supporting Microsoft 365 and Azure in a production environment. * Strong scripting and automation skills. * Experience with Microsoft Graph API (essential). * Solid understanding of M365 identity, networking, security, and monitoring. * Experience with device analytics or monitoring tools. **Soft skills:** * Demonstrated critical thinking, troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills to complex technical problems. * Excellent interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills in English to both technical and business audiences (individual or group) * Strong customer service mindset. * High\-quality documentation skills and process\-oriented approach. * Ability to complete assignments with minimal direction. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach. * **Additional competencies:*** Collaborative mindset and who can work independently or more often as part of a diverse team and group of internal and external stakeholders. * Exceptional aptitude for dealing with ambiguity, unexpected emergencies, frequent interruptions, and competing deadlines (must be able to work overtime and weekends when necessary). * Proactive, flexible and adaptable disposition with a willingness to work hands\-on, demonstrating attention to detail and the ability to take ownership of individual and team’s work. * Innate curiosity and willingness to learn and evolving technology environment on an ongoing basis: developing knowledge base and skill setting beyond that called for by immediate tasks and continually pushing the ‘knowledge envelope’. * A continuous improvement mindset, continually critically evaluating results to drive improvements of processes and / or methods and/or underlying technology direction. ### **Benefits** We reward, encourage and support our talented people to reach their full potential offering excellent progression opportunities along with a market leading remuneration package and benefits including:* Competitive salary * 25 days annual leave * Hybrid working policy * Immediate pension scheme * Comprehensive health and wellness benefits including full gym and wellness programme * TaxSaver and Bike to Work scheme * Wide\-ranging opportunities for involvement in internal committees ### **About Arthur Cox** Arthur Cox is one of Ireland’s leading law firms and provides a comprehensive service to an international client base ranging from multinational organisations, banks and financial institutions, established global leaders to government agencies and new players in emerging industry sectors. Our story has been defined by our progressive outlook. For over 100 years, the firm has led the way in the legal field in Ireland, delivering the best service and support for our clients. With an independent, entrepreneurial spirit of innovation leading the way. *Arthur Cox LLP is an equal opportunities employer. We value diversity and encourage applications from all sections of society. Please advise if you have any additional requirements, we will happily assist. Any information disclosed by you in this regard will be kept strictly confidential and shared within the firm only as necessary and with your consent.* *Reasonable accommodations provided for the purposes of your interview or which you may require during your employment will not be taken into account in hiring decisions.*

title: Network Support Specialist - Sovereign Core
description: **Introduction** At IBM Software, we transform client challenges into solutions. Building the world’s leading AI\-powered, cloud\-native products that shape the future of business and society. Our legacy of innovation creates endless opportunities for IBMers to learn, grow, and make an impact on a global scale. Working in Software means joining a team fueled by curiosity and collaboration. You’ll work with diverse technologies, partners, and industries to design, develop, and deliver solutions that power digital transformation. With a culture that values innovation, growth, and continuous learning, IBM Software places you at the heart of IBM’s product and technology landscape. Here, you’ll have the tools and opportunities to advance your career while creating software that changes the world. **Role Overview** **Your role and responsibilities** IBM Sovereign Core is shaping the future of cloud and AI, for governments, public institutions and highly regulated enterprises by placing sovereignty, governance, and trust at the center of the platform. As a Senior Customer Support Engineer in IBM Sovereign Core, you will serve as a key technical specialist responsible for supporting highly secure, sovereign cloud environments. deployed across on‑premises, partner‑operated, and fully isolated (air‑gapped) infrastructures, built on Red Hat OpenShift and integrated with IBM’s AI, automation, and governance capabilities. You will handle complex incidents, provide deep troubleshooting expertise, and work closely with customers operating in highly regulated and jurisdiction‑sensitive environments. We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Network Support Specialist to help troubleshoot large‑scale, mission‑critical secure environments. This role focuses on advanced problem resolution, customer trust, and technical depth, with growing influence across teams and environments. You will work across modern data‑center architectures, cloud‑native platforms, and highly regulated infrastructures ensuring network services are resilient, robust, and high‑performing. Operate as a senior, independent resolver for complex issues, provide technical leadership in incident handling, and partnering effectively across teams. Due to the nature of this role and the associated client requirements, which include working with EU‑based customers in a sovereign environment, applicants must, by the job start date, lawfully reside in, be legally authorized to work in, and perform the role exclusively within the EU country of employment. **Preferred Education** Bachelor's Degree **Required Technical And Professional Expertise** * Strong hands\-on troubleshooting across TCP/IP, DNS, routing, and firewall/ACL controlled environments, with the ability to isolate fault domains quickly and accurately. * Deep operational knowledge of Red Hat OpenShift/Kubernetes networking, including pod/service networking, OpenShift Routes/Ingress Controllers, and NetworkPolicy\-driven segmentation. * Experience supporting restricted and disconnected (air\-gapped) environments, including internal registries/mirrors, proxy\-controlled egress, and tightly governed network change processes. * Support and troubleshoot BGP edge and internal routing issues * Diagnose and resolve control plane and data plane failures * Support multi\-tenant isolation using VLAN, VXLAN, and EVPN * Confident communicator who can work effectively with customer network/security teams and internal engineering/SRE to resolve complex incidents under strict sovereignty and compliance constraints. This role may require candidates to contribute to a shared shift and on‑call rotation as part of collaborative incident response support. **Preferred Technical And Professional Experience** **Preferred Additional Skills** * MetalLB and Load Balancer Operations Experience configuring and troubleshooting MetalLB , including VIP management, failover behaviour, and load‑balancer troubleshooting. * ACM Multi‑Cluster Networking Experience supporting hub\-spoke network topologies for managed clusters, including cross‑cluster connectivity and consistent network‑policy enforcement across multi‑cluster environments. * NetBox IPAM / DCIM Operations Hands‑on experience with NetBox data model and API usage, network device inventory management, and integration with network automation tools.
